methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $100M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Climate & Energy (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 19 same-sector peers exist in Climate & Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Climate & Bio-Manufacturing

data.narrative:
MineSense Technologies, a Growth-stage company in the Climate & Energy sector, has raised over $100M to date. The valuation universe for Climate & Energy includes 1 public comparable (LNZA) and 8 private peers, though none achieved sufficient similarity in stage, valuation tier, or operational scale to support a reliable peer-median approach.

Stage-progression methodology applies here, using the $100M total raised as a valuation proxy and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical of Growth-stage advancement. This yields an implied valuation of $120M. The peer-median method was not applicable; while 19 same-sector companies exist publicly and privately, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-tier comparables yielded insufficient overlap (fewer than 3 datapoints). Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable noise given sector-specific capital intensity and exit multiples in frontier energy and materials.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $102M to $138M, centered on $120M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single methodological pillar. Key uncertainties include unit economics of MineSense's core technology, customer concentration and contract duration, and the company's pathway to revenue scale—factors that peer comparables would ordinarily help calibrate but which the available comp set cannot adequately address. A narrower range would require operational and financial detail beyond current scope.
